subject: Product Management
title: Theranos
description: A short lesson to help you apply Theranos.

hook.text: Elizabeth Holmes promised a device that could run 200 blood tests from a single finger prick. It was all a lie.

buildup.text: Theranos raised $700 million and was valued at $9 billion. Holmes was hailed as the next Steve Jobs.

discovery.text: The Edison device didn't work. Theranos secretly used standard lab machines and diluted samples to fake results.

twist.text: A Wall Street Journal investigation by John Carreyrou exposed the fraud. Theranos collapsed within two years.

climax.text: Holmes was convicted of fraud. Theranos became the cautionary tale of what happens when vision replaces honesty.

punchline.text: Vision without honesty isn't product management. It's fraud.

quiz.question: What was the fundamental failure at Theranos?
options: The technology didn't work and the company fraudulently covered it up
The product was too expensive for hospitals
Competitors built a better blood-testing device
